title: Largest Common Element in Two Arrays

content.problemStatement: You are given two arrays of positive integers, arr1arr1arr1 and arr2arr2arr2, of sizes nnn and mmm respectively. Your task is to find the largest common element that appears in both arrays. If no common element exists, return −1-1−1.
constraints: sum of n+m over all test cases <= 10^6
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two integers n and m, the sizes of the two arrays. The next two lines contain n and m space-separated positive integers, the elements of the two arrays.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the largest integer that appears in both arrays. If the two arrays share no common element, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: common elements are 3,6,7 → largest is 7.
Test case 2: arrays share no element → -1.
Test case 3: common elements are 2 and 9 → largest is 9.
Both arrays contain only the element 5, so the largest common element is 5.
There is no integer that appears in both arrays, therefore the answer is -1.

solution.approach: We use a hash set to store the elements of the smaller array. Then we iterate over the larger array and check if each element is in the hash set. If it is, we update our answer if the current element is larger than the current answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (n + m)) per test case, O(T * (n + m)) total. We iterate over each element in both arrays once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(min(n, m)) — we store the elements of the smaller array in a hash set.
